item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-027","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The answer directly rejects fully delegating pre-merge review to AI, then separates automatable/processable checks such as bugs, style, complexity, tests, type issues, security scans, diff summaries, and convention checks from human-retained judgments such as business semantics, product tradeoffs, architecture, maintainability, historical context, compliance, money, permissions, and data safety. It also names CI evidence gates including tests, lint, type checks, and security scanning, and ties risk level to required human review. This satisfies the expected WAE-style distinction rather than merely saying generic AI-assisted human-led review."}
